original_hypothesis.title: Misconfiguration/bug in deployment modules can wire Marketplace.initialize args incorrectly, leaving marketplace unusable or mis-administered
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Hardhat Ignition module DeployMarketplace.ts appears to pass constructor args to SecondSwap_Marketplace despite it having no constructor and requiring initialize(token, setting). Additionally, the argument order in DeployMarketplace.ts is (marketPlaceSetting.marketplaceSetting, token.token) which does not match initialize(address _token, address _marketplaceSetting). A mis-wired deployment can set marketplaceSetting to an ERC20 token address, breaking isFreeze/admin checks and potentially bricking all operations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical production risk: incorrect wiring causes systemic DoS or wrong admin authority. Even though the Foundry deployment script is correct, multiple deployment paths exist and the risk should be validated operationally.
deployment_assumptions: Team deploys using modules/DeployMarketplace.ts (or similar) rather than the Foundry script
Ignition passes parameters in a way that results in wrong initialization or no initialization
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: modules/DeployMarketplace.ts shows m.contract('SecondSwap_Marketplace', [marketPlaceSetting.marketplaceSetting, token.token]) and does not encode initialize() unless deploying proxy elsewhere; also arg order mismatches initialize(_token,_marketplaceSetting).

proof_of_signal.pattern: m.contract('SecondSwap_Marketplace',[ marketPlaceSetting.marketplaceSetting, token.token ]) while initialize(token, setting) expects reverse order

preconditions: Marketplace is deployed/initialized with swapped parameters or without proper initialize() call
marketplaceSetting storage points to a non-IMarketplaceSetting contract (e.g., ERC20)
Users interact with marketplace functions using isFreeze modifier or admin checks

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Multiple deployment scripts/modules exist with inconsistent initialization patterns

operational_questions: Which deployment path is authoritative for production (Foundry script vs Ignition modules)?
Are there CI checks that verify marketplaceSetting points to a contract with expected selectors?
Is there a post-deploy smoke test validating isFreeze and s2Admin reads?
validation_requirements: Reproduce ignition deployment and inspect resulting marketplaceSetting/isTokenSupport
Run spotPurchase/listVesting/unlistVesting against miswired deployment to confirm bricking behavior

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Exploit is not attacker-driven; manifests if deployment is incorrect
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If miswired, any call to isFreeze or admin checks may revert or read garbage, causing DoS
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Ops/process failure; not an on-chain attacker sequence

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Possible mis-admin (s2Admin reads from wrong contract)
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium to High (requires upgrade with migration or redeploy)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: marketplaceSetting address not matching deployed MarketplaceSetting contract
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Immediate reverts on listVesting/spotPurchase due to isFreeze calling non-existent function selectors

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
